title: Beehiiv vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Beehiiv and Buttondown email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which newsletter tool suits your needs.
h1: Beehiiv vs Buttondown: Detailed Comparison Guide
intro: Beehiiv and Buttondown are both popular freemium email marketing platforms, but they cater to different audiences. Beehiiv focuses on monetization and growth features for media operators, while Buttondown emphasizes simplicity and developer-friendly tools for technical writers. Choosing between them depends on your specific needs and technical preferences.

comparison_table.tool_a: Monetization and growth tools (ad network, referral program)
comparison_table.tool_b: Markdown support and API access for developers

choose_a_if: You want to monetize your newsletter through ads or paid subscriptions at scale
You're a media operator or publisher looking for growth features and audience segmentation
You need a larger free tier to test before committing to paid plans (2,500 subscribers)
choose_b_if: You prefer writing newsletters in Markdown and want developer-friendly tools
You need API access to integrate with your existing workflow or applications
You're budget-conscious and want the lowest paid tier starting at just $9/month

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your use case. Beehiiv excels for publishers seeking monetization and growth features, while Buttondown is superior for developers and technical writers who value Markdown and API access. Beehiiv has slightly higher ratings overall and more robust growth features, making it the winner for most media operators.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Beehiiv or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown is significantly cheaper, starting at $9/month compared to Beehiiv's $39/month. However, Beehiiv offers a much larger free tier (2,500 vs 100 subscribers), so the better choice depends on your current subscriber count and budget constraints.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to Beehiiv?
faq.a: Yes, Buttondown offers import/export functionality, making it possible to migrate your subscriber list and content to Beehiiv. The migration process is typically straightforward, though you may need to reconfigure some settings like automations and segments in Beehiiv's interface.
